column4: Given Kueue is enabled in the DSC and the current namespace has the kueue.x-k8s.io/managed label when a Kueue-managed InferenceService is in the pre-admission phase then the Status column displays a Kueue-specific scheduling state
column6: Table displays Kueue-specific scheduling states (Admitted, Running, Pending, Suspended, Scaling) in the Scheduling column for all Kueue-managed InferenceServices. Queue column shows assigned queue names.

column4: Given Kueue is not enabled in the DSC or the namespace is not Kueue-managed when a user views the model deployments overview then no Kueue-related indicators are displayed
column6: Source code confirms enableKueueScheduling feature flag conditionally renders Queue and Scheduling columns. Cannot visually verify disabled state — prototype has no Settings page or UI toggle for feature flags.
column10: Go to the prototype code and set enableKueueScheduling to false in FeatureFlagsContext.tsx then reload the page to confirm the Queue and Scheduling columns disappear from the deployments table

column4: Given an InferenceService with no associated Workload CR when it appears alongside Kueue-managed InferenceServices then it displays its normal KServe-derived status with no Kueue indicators
column6: Project Y Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct row displays Ready status and Unmanaged scheduling label with no Kueue-specific indicators. Row has no expand toggle (no queue details).

column4: Given a Kueue-managed InferenceService whose scheduling status changes when the user is viewing the model deployments overview then the status updates without page refresh
column6: Backend-only — real-time WebSocket updates cannot be verified in a static prototype. The UI renders from React state which would update on WebSocket message. Noted for engineering.

column4: Given a user who lacks RBAC permission to read Workload CRs when viewing the model deployments overview then deployments render with normal KServe-derived status and no error indicators
column6: Page renders cleanly with no 403 errors or broken UI elements. All deployment rows display properly with status labels and tooltips functioning.

column4: When a user hovers over the scheduling status indicator for a Kueue-managed InferenceService then a tooltip displays the requested and admitted CPU memory and GPU resources
column6: Tooltip on Admitted label shows: GPU 1 requested / 1 admitted, CPU 4 requested / 4 admitted, Memory 16Gi requested / 16Gi admitted. Resource values match Workload CR mock data.

column4: The Kueue integration covers both InferenceService and LLMInferenceService resource types with correlation logic handling the different pod discovery labels for each type
column6: Both Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct and Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.3 expandable rows show Queue Details, Resource Allocation, and Timing sections. Source code uses KueueInfo interface for both resource types.
